Not a duplicate of any other gap in this batch or sibling poc-sweep files (prototype.json/ux-scenarios.json have no marketing-page gap). proposed_issue is sane: project/milestone (ui, B2) match E2's ledger mapping; blocked_by [ENG-2109] correctly omits the E2-S3 sibling per this program's convention (same-service ordering is 'Sibling'/'Intra-service order' prose, not a structured blocked_by entry — cf. canonical E4-S3/E5-S5 bodies).

proposed_issue.title: [ui] Public marketing/landing page — hero, 5-steps, trio value-prop, marketplace preview, cross-AI reveal, dark closer
proposed_issue.milestone: B2 — App shell, routing & first-run state machine
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **visitor**, I want **the public marketing/landing page's full content — hero + glass skill showcase, a 5-step 'How it works' row, a trio 'Why Orvex' value grid, a marketplace preview strip, a cross-AI moat reveal, and a dark closer+footer** so that **I understand the product and convert before I ever sign in** (sibling of FR-UI3).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestMarketingPageRendersAllContentSections` — a component-integration test asserting `/` (anon) renders, in order: the hero (headline + lead + twin CTAs 'Start free'/'Browse the marketplace' + a live skill showcase card with NO capture-percentage meter), a 5-step how-it-works row, a trio value-prop grid, a marketplace preview grid of real featured skills, a cross-AI reveal row (ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini/Grok), and a dark closer CTA + footer — verified through rendered DOM, never a bare routing stub. *Final H1–H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given anon `/`, When it loads, Then a hero renders with headline + lead copy + 'Start free' (signup) and 'Browse the marketplace' (wall-free discover) CTAs. *Assertion: both CTAs present and routed.* [Source: POC design-artifacts/D-Design-System/screens/01-marketing.md Region B]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given the hero, When the skill showcase renders, Then it shows a real featured skill card with the honest 'well fitted · see original' residual — NEVER a 'NN% captured' meter. *Assertion: no capture-percentage string in DOM.* [Source: POC 01-marketing.md Region C + Reconciliation 'The % captured swap']
- [ ] **AC3** — Given the page, When scrolled, Then a 5-tile 'How it works' row renders (Find/Read/Make it yours/Use it anywhere/It gets to know you), each with an icon, title, and one-line body. *Assertion: 5 tiles render with distinct copy.* [Source: POC 01-marketing.md Region D]
- [ ] **AC4** — Given the page, When scrolled, Then a 3-tile 'Why Orvex' value grid renders (Your memory — private-to-you copy only, never 'shared across your team'; Company knowledge; Skills marketplace). *Assertion: 3 tiles render; the Memory tile copy asserts privacy, never sharing.* [Source: POC 01-marketing.md Region E + Reconciliation privacy-copy fix]
- [ ] **AC5** — Given the page, When scrolled, Then a marketplace preview grid renders 4 real featured skill cards with a 'See all skills →' link to Discover. *Assertion: 4 real cards + working link.* [Source: POC 01-marketing.md Region F]
- [ ] **AC6** — Given the page, When scrolled, Then a cross-AI reveal row renders the 4 supported assistants (ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini/Grok) with no fabricated metric. *Assertion: 4 glyphs render.* [Source: POC 01-marketing.md Region G]
- [ ] **AC7 (negative)** — Given a signed-in session, When `/` is hit, Then this marketing content is NEVER shown (client-redirects to `/app` per E2-S3) — the marketing content only renders anon. *Assertion: signed-in `/` never mounts this content.* [Source: POC 01-marketing.md 'WDS spec alignment'; sibling E2-S3 AC2]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestMarketingPageRendersAllContentSections` (AC1/AC3/AC4/AC5/AC6).
- [ ] GREEN: hero + showcase card with the swapped honest residual (AC1/AC2); how-it-works row (AC3); trio value grid with corrected private-Memory copy (AC4); marketplace preview grid (AC5); cross-AI reveal (AC6); anon-only gating verified against E2-S3's redirect (AC7).

## 🧠 Context

The POC's `marketing.jsx` is a full classic landing page (hero/steps/trio/preview/cross-AI/closer); the only in-program story touching `/` (E2-S3 'Front door + routing map') treats the marketing page as an opaque routing target ('the public marketing page renders') and carries zero ACs for its content. Nothing else in the ui/api/knowledge programs builds this content — it is pure front-door copy/composition, not covered by the Task Box (E2-S2, a distinct first-run surface) or by Discover (E4-S1).

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep.

## 🧪 Testing

`TestMarketingPageRendersAllContentSections` (component-integration) + unit tests on each content block. CS §5 mocking: BFF featured-skills fixture; never mock own composition.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §6 shallow view · §11 honest (no capture-%) · §3 naming; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` honesty lens (no dishonest completeness meter; Memory-privacy copy correctness).

## 🔗 References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` (FR-UI3) · POC design-artifacts/D-Design-System/screens/01-marketing.md.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: ENG-2109 (Definition Pack — the contract TAG is the dispatch gate).
- [ ] Sibling: E2-S3 (front-door routing hosts this content at anon `/`); E4-S1 (marketplace preview reuses its Skill card).

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

gaps.feature: GotBetter — piece-by-piece Base-update merge overlay (auto-apply non-conflicting maker updates + per-piece Keep-mine/Take-the-new-one/Blend-both resolution, never overwrites the user's Delta)
gaps.why_uncovered: The only build coverage of a Base-version-advanced scenario is ui E4-S3 AC5: 'a Delta whose Base advanced shows the non-destructive "updated to vN — reload" banner (unsaved Delta never clobbered)' — a flat reload notice. Nowhere is the richer merge UI ticketed: classifying maker changes into simple (auto-applied, non-conflicting with the user's touched pieces) vs conflicts (pieces the user also customised), rendering side-by-side My-changes/New-version panes per conflicting piece, and letting the user choose Keep mine / Take the new one / Blend both per piece before applying. grep for 'gotbetter'/'blend'/'keep mine' across every service's plan.json+bodies returns zero hits.

verdict.reason: Verified unticketed: zero hits in feature-map.txt; ui/bodies/E4-S3.md's AC5 is confirmed to be only the flat 'updated to vN — reload' banner, no simple/conflict classification or per-piece resolution UI anywhere. POC file design-artifacts/D-Design-System/screens/07-gotbetter.md confirmed via direct read: B1 'Updated for you · nothing to decide' (simple, non-conflicting, auto-applied), B2 'Your call' conflict cards with Keep mine/Take the new one/Blend both controls defaulting to blend, and the '!upd → return null' no-update guard all match the gap's claims exactly. Not a duplicate of any other gap in this batch or sibling poc-sweep files (no 'gotbetter'/'blend'/'keep mine' hits in prototype.json or ux-scenarios.json). proposed_issue is sane: project/milestone (ui, B4) match E4's ledger mapping; blocked_by [ENG-2109] correctly omits the E4-S3 sibling per this program's Sibling-vs-Blocked-by convention.

proposed_issue.title: [ui] GotBetter — piece-by-piece Base-update merge overlay (simple auto-apply + per-piece Keep-mine/Take-new/Blend-both)
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **user with a customised skill**, I want **a merge overlay when the maker publishes a new Base version — non-conflicting maker changes auto-applied with nothing to decide, and for each piece I've also customised a side-by-side My-changes/New-version choice (Keep mine · Take the new one · Blend both)**, so that **the maker's improvements land without ever silently overwriting my own edits** (sibling of FR-UI13/E4-S3).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestGotBetterAutoAppliesSimpleAndResolvesConflictsPerPiece` — a component-integration test asserting: (a) maker changes to pieces the user never touched are classified 'simple' and rendered under an informational 'Updated for you · nothing to decide' section with an 'Applied' tag and no decision control, (b) maker changes to pieces the user DID customise are classified 'conflicts' and rendered as side-by-side My-changes/New-version panes defaulting to Blend both, (c) choosing Keep mine / Take the new one / Blend both per conflict and hitting Apply persists exactly that resolution per piece with the user's untouched Delta pieces left intact, and (d) the user's original Delta is always recoverable (undo), verified through the resolved Delta state. *Final H1–H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a Base version advances on a skill the user has customised, When the pieces the maker changed do NOT overlap the user's touched pieces, Then those changes render under an informational, non-decision 'Updated for you · nothing to decide' list with an 'Applied' tag. *Assertion: simple changes render with no decision control.* [Source: POC design-artifacts/D-Design-System/screens/07-gotbetter.md Region B1]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given a Base version advances, When a changed piece DOES overlap a piece the user customised, Then a conflict card renders with side-by-side 'My changes' / 'The new version' panes for that piece only. *Assertion: conflict card scoped to the overlapping piece(s).* [Source: POC 07-gotbetter.md Region B2]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given a conflict card, When it opens, Then the default resolution is 'Blend both' (keeps the user's wording, folds in genuinely-new maker content) — never silently 'Take the new one'. *Assertion: default decision === blend.* [Source: POC 07-gotbetter.md 'Default decision']
- [ ] **AC4** — Given a conflict card, When the user picks Keep mine / Take the new one / Blend both, Then the choice is recorded per-piece and does not affect other conflicting pieces. *Assertion: per-piece independent decisions.* [Source: POC 07-gotbetter.md Choice segmented control]
- [ ] **AC5** — Given the resolutions, When 'Apply update' is hit, Then the Delta is updated to reflect exactly the chosen per-piece resolutions, the user's untouched Delta pieces are unchanged, and the prior Delta remains recoverable (undo/original-safe). *Assertion: `TestGotBetterAutoAppliesSimpleAndResolvesConflictsPerPiece` — resolved state matches decisions; undo path exists.* [Source: POC 07-gotbetter.md Region C safety line + Reconciliation 'Never overwrite the user's work']
- [ ] **AC6 (negative)** — Given no maker update exists for a skill, When the overlay is invoked, Then nothing renders (no update = no overlay). *Assertion: guard renders null.* [Source: POC 07-gotbetter.md 'No update / guard']

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestGotBetterAutoAppliesSimpleAndResolvesConflictsPerPiece` (AC1/AC2/AC5).
- [ ] GREEN: simple-vs-conflict piece classification against the user's touched-pieces set (AC1/AC2); side-by-side merge panes + Blend-both default (AC3); per-piece Choice control (AC4); Apply → resolved Delta + undo-safe (AC5); no-update guard (AC6).

## 🧠 Context

The only ticketed behaviour for a Base version advancing while the user has an unsaved/saved Delta is E4-S3 AC5's flat 'updated to vN — reload' banner — materially simpler than the POC's promise ('we never overwrite your work; choose what to keep — piece by piece'). The banner is a reasonable floor for the *unsaved-edit* concurrency case; this story is the richer *published-new-Base-version* merge flow the POC actually specifies, referenced from the Your-Skills library ('New version available' inline button, 10-myskills.md B3) and flagged across the pack as a Product-Owner scope call, not as settled-out — i.e. still real, unticketed product functionality if in scope.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep.

## 🧪 Testing

`TestGotBetterAutoAppliesSimpleAndResolvesConflictsPerPiece` (component-integration) + unit tests on the simple/conflict classifier. CS §5 mocking: BFF Delta + Base-diff fixtures; never mock own classifier/merge render.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §6 shallow view · §11 honest (never overwrite, undo-safe) · §5 fixture the sibling; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` no-data-loss lens.

## 🔗 References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` (FR-UI13, MB3 canon) · POC design-artifacts/D-Design-System/screens/07-gotbetter.md · 10-myskills.md B1/B3.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: ENG-2109 (Definition Pack — the contract TAG is the dispatch gate).
- [ ] Sibling: E4-S3 (Base+Delta Builder — hosts the Delta this overlay resolves into; E4-S3's simpler 'reload' banner remains the concurrent-edit floor, this is the richer new-version merge).
- [ ] PO scope confirmation: whether GotBetter ships as a baseline flow or a later power surface (per POC's own flagged open call) should be confirmed before dispatch, but the functionality is unticketed either way.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

gaps.feature: Skill Discussion — comments (with 'What I changed' delta disclosure) + suggestions (vote/status) + plain-English version history + top-contributors sidebar + community-earned-Trusted explainer
gaps.why_uncovered: api E2-S3 tickets the BACKEND social layer (publish/fork-lineage, use-gated reviews, comments/suggestions, following/profiles, append-only reputation_ledger) but there is no ui-layer story consuming it: no story in ui/plan.json renders a comments composer + comment list, a suggestion composer + vote/status list, a plain-English version-history timeline, a top-contributors sidebar, or the reputation-ladder chip. grep across every service's plan.json titles for 'discussion'/'comment'/'version history'/'top contributor' returns zero UI matches (only cli/contracts 'comment/attachment' wiki-doc plumbing and workgraph item-notes, neither of which is this skill-community surface).

verdict.reason: Verified unticketed: feature-map.txt hits for comment/discussion are only wiki-doc plumbing (cli/contracts) and workgraph item-notes, confirmed unrelated. api E2-S3 (ENG-2303) confirmed as the backend-only social layer with no ui consumer story anywhere. POC file design-artifacts/D-Design-System/screens/08-discussion.md confirmed via direct read: comments with 'What I changed' delta strip, sign-in-first composer gate (Q2/FR-27), suggestions with vote+status and explicitly NO Accept/Reject ('belongs in the J5 inbox, NOT here... missing "Review in inbox →" link... is a build gap'), version history, and the person-vs-skill 'Trusted' vocabulary-collision flag all match the gap's claims exactly. Distinct from gap 5 (Review inbox) per the POC's own text — this is the read/reply surface, J5 is the separate decision surface — so not an in-batch duplicate. FIX APPLIED: proposed_issue.blocked_by was missing ENG-2303 despite the body's own Dependencies section stating 'Blocked by: ... api E2-S3 (social API ...)' — added, matching this program's convention of structured blocked_by for explicit cross-service 'Blocked by:' dependencies (cf. ext-sweep/prd.json multi-ENG blocked_by arrays).

proposed_issue.title: [ui] Skill Discussion — comments/suggestions/version-history feedback surface + reputation-ladder chip
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **skill user**, I want **a per-skill Discussion surface — a Comments tab (with an honest 'What I changed' delta disclosure), a Suggestions tab (plain-English composer + vote/status, sign-in-first), a plain-English Version-history tab, and a top-contributors sidebar using the shared New→Contributor→Reliable→Steward reputation-ladder chip (never the skill-level 'Trusted' label reused for a person)** so that **community feedback is legible, itemised, and never an opaque score** (sibling of api E2-S3, FR-SA3).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestDiscussionTabsRenderCommentsSuggestionsHistory` — a component-integration test asserting the three tabs render from the social API (comments with optional delta-disclosure strip, suggestions with vote count + status chip + honest empty state, a version-history timeline with a 'Current' marker), the comment/suggest composers are gated behind sign-in before they open, suggestions carry no Accept/Reject controls (status + votes only — decisions live in the Review inbox), and every person-reputation signal renders via the shared ladder chip (never a person-level 'Trusted' label), verified through rendered output. *Final H1–H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a skill, When Discussion opens, Then three tabs render: Comments (with live count), Suggestions (with live count), Version history. *Assertion: 3 tabs with real counts from the API, never a fabricated count.* [Source: POC design-artifacts/D-Design-System/screens/08-discussion.md B1]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given Comments, When a comment carries a declared customisation, Then a 'What I changed: {note}' delta-disclosure strip renders on that comment only. *Assertion: strip renders iff the comment has a changed-note.* [Source: POC 08-discussion.md B3]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given the Comment/Suggest composer, When an anon visitor opens it, Then Clerk sign-in gates the composer before it opens (never assume signed-in). *Assertion: composer blocked pre-auth.* [Source: POC 08-discussion.md Reconciliation 'Sign-in-first feedback (Q2/FR-27)']
- [ ] **AC4** — Given Suggestions, When rendered, Then each item shows a vote count, a status chip (In review/Merged, text+shape not colour-alone), and NO Accept/Reject control — decision-making is out of scope here (owned by the Review inbox). *Assertion: no accept/reject affordance present; 'Review in inbox →' link shown to owners.* [Source: POC 08-discussion.md B4 + Reconciliation 'Partial-accept & one decision surface']
- [ ] **AC5** — Given zero suggestions, When the tab renders, Then an honest empty state shows ('No suggestions yet — be the first to make this skill better.'), never '0 suggestions'. *Assertion: honest empty copy, no bare zero.* [Source: POC 08-discussion.md B4 empty state]
- [ ] **AC6** — Given Version history, When rendered, Then a timeline shows each version with a plain-English maker note, and the current version is visually marked. *Assertion: timeline renders with a 'Current' marker.* [Source: POC 08-discussion.md B5]
- [ ] **AC7** — Given a contributor's reputation, When shown (sidebar top-contributors, comment author, suggestion attribution), Then it renders via the shared reputation-ladder chip (New → Contributor → Reliable → Steward), decomposable (e.g. 'N suggestions accepted across M skills') — NEVER the skill-level 'Trusted' label reused for a person. *Assertion: person signals use the ladder chip, not a person 'Trusted' pill.* [Source: POC 08-discussion.md Reconciliation 'Reputation vocabulary collision']

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestDiscussionTabsRenderCommentsSuggestionsHistory` (AC1/AC4/AC7).
- [ ] GREEN: tab bar + Comments list/composer + delta-disclosure strip (AC1/AC2); sign-in gate on composers (AC3); Suggestions list/composer, vote+status, no decision controls, 'Review in inbox →' link (AC4/AC5); Version-history timeline (AC6); shared reputation-ladder chip wired to top-contributors/comments/suggestions (AC7).

## 🧠 Context

api E2-S3 builds the backend social layer this surface reads/writes (comments/suggestions/reviews/following/reputation_ledger), but nothing in the ui program renders it. This is the Skill Detail's community/feedback face — distinct from the J5 Review-inbox decision surface (separately gapped) and from the private Memory surface (no reputation/sharing chrome ever crosses into Memory, per R1 — unaffected by this story).

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep.

## 🧪 Testing

`TestDiscussionTabsRenderCommentsSuggestionsHistory` (component-integration) + unit tests on the sign-in gate and the ladder-chip mapping. CS §5 mocking: api social-layer fixtures (E2-S3); never mock own tab/composer render.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §6 shallow view · §11 honest empty/no-opaque-score · §5 fixture the sibling; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` honesty lens (itemised reputation, never an opaque score); person-trust vs skill-Trusted collision lens.

## 🔗 References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` / `85qj2wwU2L` (FR-SA3) · POC design-artifacts/D-Design-System/screens/08-discussion.md.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: ENG-2109 (Definition Pack — the contract TAG is the dispatch gate).
- [ ] Blocked by: api E2-S3 (social API — comments/suggestions/reputation_ledger) must expose its contract at pack certification.
- [ ] Sibling: E4-S2 (Skill Viewer hosts the 'Something off?'/Discuss entry point); the separately-gapped Review-inbox (J5) story owns Accept/Reject.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

The screen doc itself flags this as unbuilt: 'No segmented control... No search-within-library/sort/filter... ADD in build.'

verdict.reason: Verified unticketed: zero hits in feature-map.txt; ui/bodies/E5-S5.md's 5 ACs cover only Delta-intact reopen, Collections CRUD, bulk export and cross-device hydrate — no segmentation/search/sort/filter/status-badge AC. ADD per spec', and the 'Card status badge... extend to the full §2.4 badge set' lines match the gap's claims verbatim. Not a duplicate of any other gap in this batch or sibling poc-sweep files. proposed_issue is sane: project/milestone (ui, B5) match E5's ledger mapping; blocked_by [ENG-2109] correctly omits the E5-S5 sibling per this program's Sibling-vs-Blocked-by convention (body says 'ship after E5-S5's base list lands' as prose ordering, not a structured blocker).

proposed_issue.title: [ui] Your Skills library — segmented Created/Saved/Drafts control + in-library search/sort/filter + full status-badge set
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **user with a growing library**, I want **a segmented All / Created / Saved / Drafts control (with live counts, empty sub-groups omitted), an in-library search box, a sort control, filter chips, and a full status badge per card (Draft / Published / Native / Forked+lineage / Saved+source)** so that **I can find and understand any of my skills at a glance, however large my library grows** (sibling of FR-UI19/E5-S5).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestLibrarySegmentsSearchSortFilterAndStatusBadges` — a component-integration test asserting the segmented control renders All/Created/Saved/Drafts with live counts and omits empty sub-groups, in-library search filters the current segment as-you-type, sort and filter chips compose with the segment and search, and every card renders the correct status badge (Draft/Published/Native/Forked with a lineage chip/Saved with a source chip) using text+shape never colour-alone, verified through rendered state. *Final H1–H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given the library, When it loads, Then a segmented control renders All / Created / Saved / Drafts with live counts inline; empty sub-groups are omitted, never shown as a fake '0' segment. *Assertion: segment counts are real; zero-count segments hidden.* [Source: POC design-artifacts/D-Design-System/screens/10-myskills.md Reconciliation]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given a segment, When the user types in the in-library search box, Then results filter live within that segment (scope label visible). *Assertion: search scoped to active segment.* [Source: POC 10-myskills.md Reconciliation]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given results, When Sort is applied, Then ordering changes accordingly and composes with any active search/filter. *Assertion: sort + search + filter compose.* [Source: POC 10-myskills.md Reconciliation]
- [ ] **AC4** — Given results, When a filter chip is applied, Then the grid narrows accordingly and composes with segment/search/sort. *Assertion: filter composes with the rest.* [Source: POC 10-myskills.md Reconciliation]
- [ ] **AC5** — Given a card, When rendered, Then its status badge is one of Draft / Published / Native / Forked (with a lineage chip to the parent) / Saved (with a source chip) — rendered as text+shape, never colour-alone. *Assertion: correct badge per skill state; text+icon present.* [Source: POC 10-myskills.md Reconciliation 'Card status badge']
- [ ] **AC6 (negative)** — Given a segment/search/filter combination with zero results, When rendered, Then an honest segment-specific empty state renders (never a blank grid, never '0'). *Assertion: `TestLibrarySegmentsSearchSortFilterAndStatusBadges` — empty combination shows guidance.* [Source: POC 10-myskills.md 'Segment-empty / filtered-empty... MUST be added']

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestLibrarySegmentsSearchSortFilterAndStatusBadges` (AC1/AC5/AC6).
- [ ] GREEN: segmented control + live counts + empty-subgroup omission (AC1); in-library search (AC2); sort (AC3); filter chips (AC4); full status-badge set (AC5); composed-empty honest state (AC6).

## 🧠 Context

E5-S5 covers Delta-intact reopen, Collections CRUD, bulk export, and cross-device hydrate for the library, but ships a flat unsegmented list with only a binary 'Made yours'/'Saved' badge (matching the POC's own flat 4-card capture). The POC's own reconciliation explicitly calls out the segmented control, in-library search/sort/filter, and the fuller status-badge set as required additions this story fills.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep.

## 🧪 Testing

`TestLibrarySegmentsSearchSortFilterAndStatusBadges` (component-integration) + unit tests on the segment/search/sort/filter composition. CS §5 mocking: BFF library fixtures (shared with E5-S5); never mock own composition logic.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §6 shallow view · §11 honest empty states (segment/filtered-empty distinct) · §5 fixture the sibling; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` honesty lens (text+shape status, never colour-alone).

## 🔗 References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` (FR-UI19) · POC design-artifacts/D-Design-System/screens/10-myskills.md.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: ENG-2109 (Definition Pack — the contract TAG is the dispatch gate).
- [ ] Sibling: E5-S5 (Library & Collections — this story extends its list surface with segmentation/search/sort/filter/status-badges; ship after E5-S5's base list lands).

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

gaps.feature: Review inbox (J5) — the skill-owner's canonical decision surface for incoming community Suggestions: Accept / Reject, including per-block partial-accept, reachable at the '/app/review' sidebar destination
gaps.source_ref: design-artifacts/D-Design-System/screens/00-app-shell.md WDS-alignment table ('Review /review — (none) — prototype TopNav has NO Review/inbox/badge — divergence') + 08-discussion.md Reconciliation ('Accept/Reject... belongs in the J5 inbox, NOT here... missing "Review in inbox →" link... is a build gap')
gaps.why_uncovered: ui E2-S1 wires '/app/review' as one of the shell's 6 sidebar destinations (route + nav item + inbox-drawer badge), but no story renders what actually lives at that route. grep for 'accept.{0,10}reject'/'partial-accept'/'review inbox'/'J5' across every service's plan.json+bodies returns only the bare route string in E2-S1 — no page content, no decision UI, anywhere in the program.

verdict.reason: Unticketed in breakdown/*/plan.json is true, but this is a cross-batch DUPLICATE within the same gap-hunt sweep: gap-hunt/poc-sweep/prototype.json already has a CONFIRMED gap 'Skill Review Inbox — owner feedback queue: comments + suggestions across owned skills, per-block diff...' grounded in POC source web/src/prototype/screens/review.jsx (784 lines) — its own verdict text explicitly notes "Milestone 'NEW' matches the established convention already used for this same J5 screen in the sibling poc-sweep/screens.json output", i.e. it already knows about and describes the SAME feature as this gap. gap-hunt/poc-sweep/ux-scenarios.json independently proposes a third near-identical ticket, 'Review inbox (J5) unified queue at /review — per-block Accept/Reject + Accept-all/Reject-all with partial-accept... contributor reputation ladder'. Filing all three would create duplicate Linear issues for one feature. Dropping this screens.json instance in favor of the prototype.json/ux-scenarios.json versions, which are grounded in richer/more-detailed POC sources (live prototype code vs. this gap's digested design-doc citations). Secondary defects also found in this instance's proposed_issue, moot given the duplicate finding but noted for the record: milestone 'NEW — Review inbox (J5 decision surface)' breaks this program's own convention of attaching net-new gap scope to an existing epic as '<epic>-GAPn' (cf. E1-GAP1/E3-GAP1/E6-GAP1/E7-GAP1/E8-GAP1, none of which spawn a bespoke top-level milestone) — E2-GAP1 under B2 was free and a better fit since /app/review is one of E2-S1's own 6 shell destinations; and blocked_by [ENG-2109] omits ENG-2658 (ui E2-S1) and ENG-2303 (api E2-S3) despite the body's own Dependencies section stating 'Blocked by: E2-S1 ... api E2-S3 ...'.

proposed_issue.title: [ui] Review inbox (J5) — skill-owner Accept/Reject decision surface with per-block partial-accept
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **skill owner**, I want **the `/app/review` inbox to list incoming community Suggestions on my skills and let me Accept, Reject, or partially-accept a suggestion at per-block granularity — with the Original⇄With-this-change diff and the contributor's reputation-ladder chip visible before I decide** so that **the merge decision has one canonical, honest home instead of being scattered across skill pages** (sibling of FR-UI1/E2-S1, api FR-SA3).

**Definition of Done:** ONE named test `TestReviewInboxAcceptRejectPartialAcceptPerBlock` — a component-integration test asserting `/app/review` lists pending Suggestions across the owner's skills with the contributor's reputation-ladder chip and an Original⇄With-this-change diff per suggestion, Accept/Reject act on the whole suggestion, per-block partial-accept lets the owner accept a subset of a multi-block suggestion while rejecting the rest, every decision emits the itemised append-only reputation-ledger effect (accept → credit, reject → no clawback, revert-later → clawback per api E2-S3), and the inbox badge count is honest ('—' while loading, absent at zero, never a stale/fabricated number), verified through rendered state + the emitted decision payload. *Final H1–H17 elaboration + exact contract tag/versions are pinned at pack certification (ENG-2109); this story is dispatch-blocked until that tag exists.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a signed-in skill owner, When `/app/review` loads, Then it lists pending Suggestions across all of the owner's skills, each showing the skill title, the suggestion title/note, and the contributor's reputation-ladder chip. *Assertion: list renders with real per-item attribution.* [Source: POC design-artifacts/D-Design-System/screens/00-app-shell.md IA mapping ('Review `/review`') + 08-discussion.md 'Review in inbox →' gap]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given a suggestion, When opened, Then an Original ⇄ With-this-change diff renders before any decision is made. *Assertion: diff view present pre-decision.* [Source: POC 08-discussion.md Reconciliation 'one canonical decision surface (J5)']
- [ ] **AC3** — Given a single-block suggestion, When the owner clicks Accept or Reject, Then the whole suggestion is accepted/rejected and the skill's published Base updates (Accept) or nothing changes (Reject). *Assertion: whole-suggestion accept/reject works.* [Source: POC 08-discussion.md]
- [ ] **AC4** — Given a multi-block suggestion, When the owner reviews it, Then each block can be independently accepted or rejected (partial-accept), and only the accepted blocks land in the published Base. *Assertion: partial-accept persists a subset.* [Source: POC 08-discussion.md Reconciliation 'incl. per-block partial-accept']
- [ ] **AC5** — Given a decision, When it resolves, Then the append-only `reputation_ledger` (api E2-S3) receives the itemised effect (accept → credited-merge count; reject → no ledger credit) — never an in-place score mutation. *Assertion: ledger call shape matches E2-S3's append-only contract.* [Source: api E2-S3 AC4]
- [ ] **AC6** — Given the sidebar inbox badge (E2-S1 AC3), When pending count is loading/zero, Then it shows '—' while loading and is absent at zero — never a stale or fabricated number. *Assertion: badge honesty holds end-to-end from this surface's data.* [Source: POC 00-app-shell.md 'Honest empty states' + E2-S1 AC3]
- [ ] **AC7 (negative)** — Given zero pending suggestions, When the inbox loads, Then an honest empty state renders ('Nothing to review yet.'), never a fabricated queue. *Assertion: `TestReviewInboxAcceptRejectPartialAcceptPerBlock` — empty-queue path renders honest copy.* [Source: house honest-empty rule]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestReviewInboxAcceptRejectPartialAcceptPerBlock` (AC1/AC4/AC5).
- [ ] GREEN: inbox list + reputation-ladder chip + diff (AC1/AC2); whole-suggestion Accept/Reject (AC3); per-block partial-accept (AC4); ledger-effect wiring (AC5); honest badge (AC6); honest empty queue (AC7).

## 🧠 Context

`/app/review` is one of the shell's 6 sidebar destinations per E2-S1, but E2-S1 only wires the route and a badge — it explicitly does not build the page content. The POC never captured this screen directly (it isn't one of the 11 digested design-system screens), but every skill-community screen that IS captured (08-discussion, and by extension 04-skillviewer) explicitly routes the Accept/Reject decision here and flags its absence as a build gap: 'Accept/Reject (incl. per-block partial-accept) belongs in the J5 inbox, NOT here... the missing "Review in inbox →" link... is a build gap to add.' No story anywhere builds the inbox itself.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** POC completeness sweep.

## 🧪 Testing

`TestReviewInboxAcceptRejectPartialAcceptPerBlock` (component-integration) + unit tests on the partial-accept block selector. CS §5 mocking: api social-layer fixtures (E2-S3 reputation_ledger + suggestions); never mock own inbox render/decision logic.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §6 shallow view · §11 honest badge/empty state · §5 fixture the sibling; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` honesty lens (append-only ledger, no opaque score, no fabricated counts).

## 🔗 References

PRD `xsRMrju3D1` (FR-UI1) / `85qj2wwU2L` (FR-SA3) · POC design-artifacts/D-Design-System/screens/00-app-shell.md + 08-discussion.md.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: ENG-2109 (Definition Pack — the contract TAG is the dispatch gate).
- [ ] Blocked by: E2-S1 (sidebar/route/badge scaffold this inbox mounts into); api E2-S3 (reputation_ledger + suggestions contract this inbox decides against).
- [ ] Sibling: the separately-gapped Discussion feedback surface (comments/suggestions read-view) links here via 'Review in inbox →'.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.
